reasoning:
Kaylee McKeown currently holds the 50m backstroke long course world record (26.86, set Oct 2023). The record is very fast (0.24s ahead of others), meaning it's highly durable. However, Regan Smith has been in remarkable form, breaking the 100m LC backstroke WR in June 2024 and the 50m SC backstroke WR in Dec 2024. Thus, Smith (or potentially Berkoff) presents a credible threat in 2025. 
The May resolution dates occur before the major taper meets (US and Australian Trials are traditionally in June), so the probability she holds it remains extremely high (95-97%). By the July 26 date, the Trials will have passed and the 2025 World Championships in Singapore (July 11 - Aug 3) will be underway, introducing significant risk of the record falling (lowering p to ~0.73). The Oct 24 date falls after the World Championships and early World Cups, when all taper opportunities for 2025 have concluded, dropping the probability further to ~0.63. Remember that if McKeown lowers the record herself, she remains the record holder.
